title: Race-lap ranking model blended by rank averaging
summary: ROC AUC depends only on ordering, so a model trained to rank likely pitters within each race-lap context can complement probability models.
rationale: ROC AUC depends only on ordering, so a model trained to rank likely pitters within each race-lap context can complement probability models. XGBoost and LightGBM both support pairwise/listwise ranking objectives over grouped samples, which is a natural adjacent-competition pattern for AUC-heavy tabular tasks.
implementation_hint: Train a second model with groups defined by Year-Race-LapNumber so each lap ranks drivers by immediate pit likelihood. Use binary labels as relevance, test `rank:pairwise` or `rank:ndcg`, then blend its OOF ranks with CatBoost/XGBoost classifier OOF ranks instead of raw probabilities. Evaluate pure rank-average and a small stacked blend.
expected_effect: Can improve ordering near ambiguous pit-wave laps even if calibration worsens, which is acceptable for ROC AUC.
risk: Some groups may have zero positives, and ranking objectives can be unstable on sparse groups; implementation must skip or downweight empty-information groups.
